mission.title: Install and Configure Qdrant
mission.description: Qdrant is a high-performance vector database designed for AI applications, enabling efficient storage and retrieval of vector embeddings. It is ideal for use cases involving semantic search, recommendation systems, and neural network applications. Key trade-offs include the need for careful configuration to optimize performance and storage management.

steps.title: Add Qdrant Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Qdrant Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Qdrant using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add qdrant https://qdrant.github.io/qdrant-helm

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official Qdrant Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Qdrant with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the Qdrant database using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

helm install qdrant qdrant/qdrant --namespace qdrant --create-namespace --version 1.17.0

```

> Installs Qdrant in the 'qdrant' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Custom Resource Definition (CRD)
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource definition for Qdrant, which is necessary for managing Qdrant instances.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/crds/qdrant_crd.yaml

```

> Applies the CRD for Qdrant.

steps.title: Verify Qdrant Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Qdrant pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

```

> Lists all pods in the Qdrant namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Create Example Qdrant Collection
steps.description:
This step creates an example collection in Qdrant to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

curl -X POST 'http://<QDRANT_SERVICE_IP>:6333/collections' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name": "example_collection", "vector_size": 128}'

```

> Replace <QDRANT_SERVICE_IP> with the actual service IP.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Qdrant
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Qdrant installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in Qdrant.

```bash

helm uninstall qdrant --namespace qdrant

kubectl delete namespace qdrant

```

> Uninstalls Qdrant from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Qdrant
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade Qdrant to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl cp qdrant-pod:/var/lib/qdrant/storage ./backup_storage

helm upgrade qdrant qdrant/qdrant --namespace qdrant --version 1.17.0

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

```

> Backup the storage from the Qdrant pod.
> Upgrades Qdrant to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Qdrant'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant service may not be running or accessible.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Qdrant pods with 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Collection not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified collection does not exist in Qdrant.

**Fix:**
Verify the collection name and create it if it does not exist.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient storage space'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The storage path may be full or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the storage configuration in the Qdrant config and ensure there is sufficient disk space.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Invalid configuration'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant configuration file may contain syntax errors.

**Fix:**
Review the configuration file for errors and correct them.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for advanced filtering capabilities and improved performance for large datasets.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Qdrant service running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant' showing the status as 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
